beats.activity: Wake up, make espresso, review the day ahead knowing transit is down until 4pm.
beats.reasoning: Sebastian starts his day with his ritual espresso at home. The transit strike means he can't take his usual bus to Kirkland, so he's mentally shifting gears—low energy yesterday (40/100) carries into a slower morning as he processes the logistics.

beats.activity: Drive own car to Kirkland workplace to check in with dispatch and coordinate alternative arrangements due to strike.
beats.reasoning: With Metro buses down, Sebastian needs to drive himself to work. As a bus driver, he'd check in with dispatch to understand the day's situation and whether he's needed for contingency routes or can take the day off.

beats.activity: Meet with dispatch; learn that no service is running—day is effectively off. Grab a coffee and chat with coworkers.
beats.reasoning: Strike day means no scheduled runs. Sebastian gets an unexpected day off, which suits his laid-back nature. He'd socialize briefly with coworkers before heading out to make use of the free time.

beats.activity: Drive back to Magnolia to regroup and decide on the day.
beats.reasoning: With the day unexpectedly free and low energy carryover, Sebastian heads home to settle in and figure out what to do. Mild weather and partly cloudy skies make it a good day to be flexible.

beats.activity: Light breakfast, walk around Magnolia neighborhood, enjoy the mild morning.
beats.reasoning: With the strike keeping him grounded, Sebastian takes a solo walk in his neighborhood. Partly cloudy and 68°F is perfect for an introverted, low-energy day. He's not one for big crowds anyway.

beats.activity: Errands: stop at QFC for groceries and supplies.
beats.reasoning: Strike day is a good time to handle neighborhood errands without the usual time pressure. Stays local and low-key.

beats.activity: Lunch at home: simple sandwich and hydration.
beats.reasoning: Sebastian prefers home meals and doesn't care for crowds. On a strike day with no commute pressure, he eats at home and recharges.

beats.activity: Rest and leisure at home: read, listen to music, low-key afternoon.
beats.reasoning: Still carrying low energy (40/100) from yesterday. Introverted nature means he's comfortable spending the afternoon at home. No need to push himself on an unplanned day off.

beats.activity: Check in with a close friend via text; arrange an evening walk or casual hangout since transit is back online at 4pm.
beats.reasoning: By 4pm, buses resume service. Sebastian values time with close friends over big crowds. A casual evening walk in Wallingford (where he felt good at the concert yesterday) with one trusted friend fits his mood and personality.

beats.activity: Drive to Wallingford to meet friend.
beats.reasoning: Buses are back online at 4pm, but Sebastian drove today; he continues by car to meet his friend in Wallingford, a neighborhood where he felt grounded yesterday.

beats.activity: Evening walk and casual conversation with close friend; grab a drink (not drip coffee—maybe tea or a craft beverage).
beats.reasoning: Low-key evening with one trusted friend. Mild weather and partly cloudy conditions make for a pleasant walk. Sebastian's introverted nature and recent good feelings mean he's open to modest social time, not a big night out.
After an evening with a friend, Sebastian heads home to wind down. Across-town drive from Wallingford to Magnolia, accounting for evening traffic.

beats.activity: Wind down at home: prepare a simple dinner, listen to music, relax before bed.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down at home before sleep. Sebastian settles in for the night, staying true to his introverted nature and the low-energy carry-over from yesterday.
Overnight sleep spanning into the next calendar day, ending by 6am. A full 8.5 hours allows Sebastian to recover from low energy and be ready for a normal Friday routine.

world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
